mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output, which raises corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 20-day SPX forward returns is negative or below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, equity markets discount imminent monetary or fiscal intervention and labor market mean reversion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when UNRATE > 8%.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between month-over-month changes in CPIAUCSL and the level of FEDFUNDS predicts S&P 500 returns because accelerating inflation alongside low policy rates erodes real discount rates, channeling capital into equities over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In a regression of 20-day SPX returns on CPIAUCSL changes, FEDFUNDS levels, and their interaction, the interaction term carries a negative coefficient or t-stat below 1.5 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts remain below 600 thousand units, month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals resilient consumer spending that outweighs weak construction activity, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 600, RSAFS monthly change exhibits a negative Rank IC with 60-day SPX forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) reduce real corporate earnings expectations and tighten household purchasing power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly change shows a positive Rank IC with 5-day SPX forward returns exceeding 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% signals a liquidity trap where conventional monetary policy loses efficacy, prompting equity investors to price in deflation risk and weak aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirm a broad-based economic recovery, generating stronger positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days than either indicator alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term of PAYEMS and INDPRO monthly changes does not significantly improve prediction of 20-day SPX returns (F-test p > 0.05) relative to individual series over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal accelerating construction activity and upstream demand for materials and labor, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST monthly change exhibits a negative Rank IC with 5-day SPX forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.
